epigraph.text: For everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven.
epigraph.quote: Factorial designs allow us to study interactions between factors.

content.text:
Two-way ANOVA examines two factors simultaneously, including possible 
interactions.

content.title: Two-Way ANOVA Model
content.content:
\[
 Y_{ijk} = \mu + \alpha_i + \beta_j + (\alpha\beta)_{ij} + \epsilon_{ijk}
\]
where:

- $\alpha_i$ = effect of Factor A at level $i$

- $\beta_j$ = effect of Factor B at level $j$

- $(\alpha\beta)_{ij}$ = interaction effect

items: $H_0$: No main effect of Factor A
$H_0$: No main effect of Factor B
$H_0$: No interaction between A and B

content.text:
An **interaction** occurs when the effect of 
one factor depends on the level of the other.

Example: A drug may be effective for young patients but not old ones. 
Age and drug interact.

**Interaction plots** visualize this: parallel lines suggest no 
interaction; non-parallel lines suggest interaction.

content.title: Interpreting Interaction
content.problem:
Studying plant growth with two factors: fertilizer 
(A, B) and water level (low, high). If Fertilizer A works well only 
with high water, there's an interaction between fertilizer and water.
